steps.title: Find the yearly multiplier
steps.content:
To find the factor by which the value changes each year, evaluate the function at $x=12$ months (one year).
$$ f(12)=4200(0.42)^{12/12}=4200(0.42)^1=4200\cdot0.42 $$
This shows that after one year the value is $0.42$ times its value at the start of the year.

steps.title: Compute the annual percent decrease
steps.content:
If the value each year is $0.42$ of the previous year's value, then it has decreased by $1-0.42=0.58$ of the previous year's value.
$$ \text{Decrease fraction}=0.58 $$
Converting to a percentage gives $p=0.58\times100=58$.
D is correct.
